title: Interpreting Node Conditions
description:
## Interpreting Node Conditions

You run `kubectl describe node worker-1` and see the following in the `Conditions` section:

```
Type                 Status  Reason
----                 ------  ------
MemoryPressure       True    KubeletHasSufficientMemory
DiskPressure         True    KubeletHasSufficientDisk
PIDPressure          False   KubeletHasSufficientPID
Ready                False   KubeletNotReady
```

What is the most likely cause of this node's `Ready=False` condition, and what should you investigate first?

options.text: The node's container runtime (containerd/docker) has stopped â€” check `systemctl status containerd` on the node
The kubelet service has failed or lost connection to the API server â€” check `systemctl status kubelet` on the node
The node is out of disk space â€” check `df -h` on the node
A NetworkPolicy is blocking communication between the node and the control plane

explanation: `Ready=False` with reason `KubeletNotReady` means the **kubelet** is either not running or cannot reach the API server. The kubelet is responsible for sending heartbeat `NodeReady` conditions â€” if it stops, the node goes `NotReady` after the `node-monitor-grace-period` (default 40s). First check `systemctl status kubelet` on the affected node. `MemoryPressure=True` and `DiskPressure=True` would cause evictions but not a `NotReady` condition unless resources are critically exhausted. A NetworkPolicy cannot block kubelet-to-API-server traffic (that's host-network traffic, not pod network).

hints.title: Common NotReady causes
hints.body: 1. kubelet stopped (systemctl status kubelet). 2. Network partition between node and API server. 3. Container runtime crashed. 4. Certificate expired (check kubelet logs). Start with: journalctl -u kubelet -n 50
hints.command: kubectl get nodes -o wide && kubectl describe node worker-1 | grep -A 15 Conditions

hints.title: Checking node conditions programmatically
hints.body: Use jsonpath to extract condition status for a specific type.
